data.title: SBI Holdings reports FY results

data.title: Super League Enterprise announces pricing of public offering
data.description:
• Super League Enterprise (NASDAQ:SLE) announced on Friday the pricing of a firm commitment underwritten public offering, with gross proceeds to the company expected to be approximately $869,999.
  • The offering consists of 5,117,647 shares of common stock.
  • The public offering price per share of common stock is $0.17. In addition, the company has granted the underwriter an overallotment option to purchase up to an additional 767,647 shares of common stock, representing 15% of the number of shares offered, which option is exercisable in whole or in part at any time up to 45 days after the date of this prospectus.
  • Aggregate gross proceeds to the company are expected to be approximately $869,999, or $1 million if the over allotment option is exercised in full.
  • The transaction is expected to close on or about May 12, 2025.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from the offering, together with its existing cash, for general corporate purposes and working capital and the repayment of a portion of its indebtedness.
  • SLE -45.60% premarket to $0.177
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SRx Health Solutions purchases $1.5 million of Solana tokens
data.description:
• SRx Health Solutions (NYSE:SRXH) announced on Friday purchasing Solana Tokens (SOL) for approximately $1.5 million as part of its previously announced asset diversification strategy to allocate up to 10% of future cash flows and cash reserves into cryptocurrencies and precious metals, including Bitcoin (BTC), Solana (SOL), as well as physical gold and silver.
  • SRXH +96.22% premarket to $1.04.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: WhiteHawk to acquire PHX Minerals in $187M deal
data.description:
PHX Minerals (NYSE:PHX) +21.2% pre-market Friday after natural gas mineral and royalty company WhiteHawk agreed to acquire the company for $4.35/share in cash, a 21.8% premium to PHX’s closing price on May 7, for a total value of ~$187M including debt.

WhiteHawk said the deal will add 1.8M gross unit acres of premier natural gas mineral and royalty assets, significantly expanding its footprint in the core of the Haynesville shale in Texas and Louisiana and diversifying its portfolio into the SCOOP/STACK region in Oklahoma.

Following the deal closing, WhiteHawk will own royalty interests across ~3.1M gross unit acres, with cash flow from 10,163 producing wells, 368 wells-in-progress, 330 permitted wells and more than 7,250 undeveloped locations across its portfolio, on a pro forma basis.

data.title: Game developers: Seeking Alpha analysts vs Wall Street
data.description:
The gaming industry had an unremarkable 2024, with $58.7 billion recorded in total consumer spending, compared to $59.3 billion spent in 2023, according to the Entertainment Software Association.

Spending on mobile games continued to rise in 2024 and accounted for about half of all video game content spending at $26 billion. Meanwhile, hardware and console sales fell in 2024. 

Looking ahead, technological advancements, next-generation hardware like Nintendo Switch 2, major releases like Grand Theft Auto VI, and esports are expected to drive industry growth.

In such a prevailing environment, here's how Seeking Alpha analysts' and Wall Street opinions stack up on video game makers.

As seen in the chart above, Wall Street has more Buy or higher recommendations on the sector, while Seeking Alpha analysts carried a higher number of Sell or lower calls overall.

Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ:TTWO), Roblox Corporation (NYSE:RBLX), and Unity Software (NYSE:U) attracted a wide variety of opinions from both sides. 

Sell-side's enthusiasm for Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Sony Group (NYSE:SONY), Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOY), and NetEase (NASDAQ:NTES) was matched by Seeking Alpha analysts.

Meanwhile, Sea Limited (NYSE:SE) and Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) saw mixed opinions from SA. Wall Street was upbeat on the former and neutral on the latter.

data.title: Puma Biotechnology targets $192M-$198M NERLYNX revenue for 2025 amid strategic efforts
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Puma Biotechnology (PBYI) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Alan Auerbach reported total revenue of $46.0 million for Q1 2025, including $43.1 million in net product revenue from NERLYNX sales. This represented a decrease from the previous quarter due to seasonal inventory reductions and higher gross-to-net expenses.
  • NERLYNX sales volume was 2,338 bottles, with new prescriptions increasing by 6% quarter-over-quarter, although total prescriptions decreased by 9%.
  • The company highlighted progress on clinical trials, including updated Phase I data on the combination of neratinib and Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan, showing promising results in patients with metastatic solid tumors. Part 2 of the study has opened enrollment with data expected later in 2025.
  • Chief Financial Officer Maximo Nougues stated that "net income for Q1 2025 was $3 million or $0.06 per share," compared to $19.3 million in Q4 2024, influenced by the absence of a deferred tax benefit recorded in the prior quarter.

Outlook

  • Puma Biotechnology reaffirmed its FY2025 guidance for NERLYNX net product revenue in the range of $192 million to $198 million. Royalty revenue is expected between $20 million and $24 million, reflecting lower shipments to China due to regulatory transitions.
  • For Q2 2025, the company anticipates net product revenue of $48 million to $50 million and royalty revenue of $2 million to $3 million. Gross-to-net adjustments are projected between 20% and 21.5%, with net income estimated at $4 million to $6 million.
  • The company also expects to present more interim data from ongoing trials for alisertib in both breast and lung cancers in late 2025.

Financial Results

  • NERLYNX revenue showed a sequential decline from $54.4 million in Q4 2024 to $43.1 million in Q1 2025 due to seasonal inventory effects and higher gross-to-net adjustments of 20.8%.
  • Cost of sales dropped to $10.6 million in Q1 2025, including $2.4 million related to amortization of intangible assets.
  • Cash reserves stood at $93 million as of March 31, 2025, with a reduction in total outstanding debt to $56 million following a principal loan payment.
  • Research and development expenses decreased to $13.8 million in Q1 2025 compared to $15.2 million in the prior quarter, while SG&A expenses increased slightly to $17.6 million.

Q&A

  • Divya Rao, Cowen: Questioned the location of alisertib intellectual property and manufacturing. CEO Alan Auerbach responded that IP is licensed from Takeda, with manufacturing currently in the U.S.
  • Kun Wang, Barclays: Inquired about interim data and dose adjustments for the alisertib lung cancer trial. CEO Auerbach confirmed that the dose protocol is being amended and data is still expected later in the year.
  • Ed White, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about persistence and compliance trends for NERLYNX therapy. Chief Commercial Officer Jeff Ludwig detailed a focus on dose escalation and patient support to improve therapy duration.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed interest in detailed clinical trial data and compliance improvements but raised concerns about revenue consistency in international markets.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing ongoing efforts to support NERLYNX sales and enhance clinical trial progress.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone showed a slight defensive shift due to lower financial results and inventory adjustments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • NERLYNX net product revenue declined from $54.4 million in Q4 2024 to $43.1 million in Q1 2025, with inventory reductions cited as a primary factor.
  • Gross-to-net adjustments increased from 18.2% to 20.8%, reflecting seasonal and market dynamics.
  • Management remains optimistic about the ongoing clinical trials and future revenue growth, despite near-term challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • Seasonal inventory reductions and gross-to-net adjustments impacted financial results.
  • Lower anticipated royalty revenue from China due to regulatory changes poses a risk to FY2025 projections.
  • Analysts raised questions about the impact of Medicare Part D redesign and potential effects on patient affordability and compliance.

Final Takeaway

Puma Biotechnology emphasized its focus on improving NERLYNX utilization and advancing its clinical pipeline while managing financial challenges from inventory fluctuations and international market dynamics. The company reaffirmed its 2025 revenue guidance, signaling confidence in its strategic initiatives and ongoing clinical developments.
